title: Structures, Unions, Tagged Variants, and Flexible Arrays · Memory Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted tagged packet representation, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in tagged packet representation and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Initialize, copy, compare, and validate structures by members and invariants rather than raw-byte assumptions.
Pair a union with an explicit discriminant and access only the active, validated member.
Allocate flexible-array objects with checked header-plus-elements arithmetic and record their element count.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Aggregate Invariants
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug native-systems team handles memory case 7306. Produce bounded C evidence to validate a tagged payload and its allocation size; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Initialize, copy, compare, and validate structures by members and invariants rather than raw-byte assumptions.

Inspect the Aggregate Invariants trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Opaque Structure Evolution
